Scenario.title: Transportation System Cyber Espionage Campaign
Scenario.overview: Transportation Systems Complex A sophisticated multi-stage attack targeting transportation infrastructure leverages AI-driven reconnaissance, phishing, and zero-day exploits in SCADA systems to compromise critical operations and cause service disruptions.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Open Source Intelligence (OSINT), Process, Windows Registry, Domain Name Registrar, OS Process, Windows Service, Network Traffic, File
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Artificial Intelligence, Process Creation (Mechanism), Delivery Method, Application Log Content (Mechanism), Windows Registry Key Creation (Mechanism), Pass the Hash, Process Injection, Keylogging, Reg.exe Utility, Network Traffic Flow (Mechanism), File Modification (Mechanism), Process Creation (Mechanism), File Creation (Mechanism)

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.description: The attacker uses AI algorithms to analyze publicly available data about transportation infrastructure, identifying vulnerable SCADA systems and potential targets for exploitation.

stage.name: Resource Development
stage.capec: CAPEC-670: Software Development Tools Maliciously Altered
stage.description: The threat actor creates phishing kits using online templates to deploy malware payloads tailored for transportation system endpoints.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: Fake vendor emails with malicious attachments are sent to employees, tricking recipients into enabling initial access to the network.

stage.name: Execution
stage.description: Outdated SCADA system vulnerabilities are exploited via log injection to execute arbitrary code on critical infrastructure components.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.description: A persistent scheduled task is created in the Windows Registry to maintain access after initial compromise.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.capec: CAPEC-461: Web Services API Signature Forgery Leveraging Hash Function Extension Weakness
stage.description: Stolen credentials are used to escalate privileges and gain administrative control over network resources.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.description: Malicious code is injected into legitimate processes to evade detection mechanisms.

stage.name: Credential Access
stage.description: A keylogger is deployed to steal login credentials from infected devices during post-compromise activities.

stage.name: Discovery
stage.description: Network traffic is monitored using Reg.exe to map internal network topology and identify high-value targets.

stage.name: Lateral Movement
stage.description: Remote Desktop Protocol (RDP) is abused to move laterally across the network and compromise additional systems.

stage.name: Collection
stage.capec: CAPEC-675: Retrieve Data from Decommissioned Devices
stage.description: Operational data is extracted from control systems through file manipulation to exfiltrate sensitive information.

stage.name: Exfiltration
stage.description: Data is stealthily transferred over domain names using process creation techniques to avoid traditional monitoring.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: Malware is embedded in IIS components to disrupt critical infrastructure operations and cause service outages.

Scenario.summary: This complex attack chain demonstrates how low-skill actors can leverage AI, phishing, and known vulnerabilities in transportation systems to achieve long-term persistence, escalate privileges, and cause operational disruption. The attack highlights the importance of securing legacy systems and implementing robust endpoint detection capabilities.
